summary: Frontend-only verification of Phase 2 HUD uplift: Advisor + MyPc polish and PageHeader swaps on Builds/Upgrade/Commands/Bios/Admin. All required data-testids render on each page; PageHeader eyebrow/title/actions display correctly; AI Advisor completes a real streamed round-trip; MyPc renders Health Ring + Benchmark + Hardware + Startup with 3 hud-tick corner accents; IT<->EN toggle clean (no raw i18n keys) on Advisor and MyPc; no React runtime/pageerror across the tested pages.

test_results.advisor_page: PASS (specs-badge visible with 'Tips personalized to your PC: AMD Ryzen 7 5800X · NVIDIA RTX 3070'; suggestion-0..3 rendered with icon+hover styling; new-chat-btn present; chat-input + chat-send-btn present; PageHeader // AI ADVISOR eyebrow + 'Your PC optimization expert' title)
test_results.advisor_chat_roundtrip: PASS (typed 'test' + send -> streaming reply completed; assistant bubble rendered via ReactMarkdown; body grew to 1684 chars; no crash; note: .typing-dot rendered only while streaming — was not captured in a poll at 1.5s in this run but code path confirmed in Advisor.jsx L202-205)
test_results.advisor_ai_avatar: PASS (assistant bubbles render the MessageSquareCode 7x7 avatar div — see Advisor.jsx L192-194; visible in screenshot as cyan bordered square)
test_results.mypc_page: PASS (PageHeader // MY PC + 'PC analysis' + edit-specs-btn/to-upgrade-btn/refresh-pc-btn; Health Score ring shows 95 STRONG in green; Benchmark card with deltas +30%/+30%/+20%; Hardware list; Startup Programs w/ Analyze with AI button; 3 hud-tick corner accents present)
test_results.upgrade_page: PASS (PageHeader renders, page loads without console error)
test_results.commands_page: PASS (PageHeader with subtitle renders; command categories load; only false positives in raw-key scan are actual filenames/URLs like powershell.exe / wsreset.exe)
test_results.bios_page: PASS (PageHeader with subtitle renders; page loads; only 'e.g' false positive in raw-key scan)
test_results.admin_page: PASS (PageHeader with shield eyebrow 'ADMIN' + 'Administrator panel' title; 4 stat cards (users 31, admins 2, products 11, builds 0); users table with role/delete action buttons per row)
test_results.i18n_switch_advisor: PASS (no raw advisor.* keys after IT toggle then EN toggle)
test_results.i18n_switch_mypc: PASS (no raw pc.* / mypc.* keys after IT toggle then EN toggle)
test_results.console_errors: Only a benign 401 on unauthenticated /me before login. No React errors, no pageerror, no CRA overlay across the 7 tested pages.

critical_code_review_comments: Advisor.jsx assistant avatar is a raw MessageSquareCode-in-div without a data-testid — consider adding data-testid="ai-avatar" to make automated verification of the review requirement ('assistant messages show an AI avatar') trivial. Currently must be verified by visual/CSS inspection.

Advisor.jsx typing indicator (.typing-dot) is only mounted while streaming with empty content; on fast responses it can be missed by pollers. Consider adding a stable data-testid="typing-indicator" wrapper span so tests can assert the state directly.

MyPc PageHeader actions use edit-specs-btn/to-upgrade-btn/refresh-pc-btn — all present. Health ring, benchmark and hardware panels all render with hud-tick corners as intended (3 detected on desktop layout).

Header swaps on BuildGenerator/Upgrade/Commands/BiosRestore/Admin did not regress any of the existing critical data-testids (generate-build-btn confirmed; role/delete actions on Admin still rendered per row).
seed_data_creation: One 'test' message sent to AI Advisor in a new session; leaves an extra session in the sidebar list but is otherwise harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 HUD uplift is stable. Advisor + MyPc render as designed with the shared PageHeader. Nice-to-haves for future rounds: (1) add data-testid to the assistant avatar and typing-indicator in Advisor.jsx to allow direct assertions; (2) MyPc edit-specs-btn opens a form but the form root is not a native <form> element (query for 'form' returned null) — verify open/close flow via SpecsForm testids if you need to automate saving specs.
